title: The Snubs and Surprises of the 92nd Academy Award Nominations | The Oscars Show
format: Sean and Amanda go through the 92nd Oscar nominations morning-of: Joker's surprise lead, Parasite's six-nomination haul, and who the Academy left out.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Led all films with 11 nominations off a single acting nod (Joaquin Phoenix), spread across directing, writing, and the crafts.
Tied for 10 nominations; the hosts had pegged it as the front-runner for below-the-line categories.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: 10 nominations, unsurprising.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: 10 nominations, unsurprising.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: 6 nominations, including Best Picture and Best Director for Bong Joon-ho, but no acting nominations.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: 6 nominations and a Best Picture nod, but Greta Gerwig missed Best Director.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: 6 nominations for Driver, Johansson, Picture, and Screenplay, but no Best Director or Editing nod for Noah Baumbach.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: 3 below-the-line nominations, including John Williams' 52nd, despite the hosts not rating the film itself.

films.title: The Two Popes
films.note: 4 nominations including lead notices for both Jonathan Pryce and Anthony Hopkins, the latter placed in supporting.

films.title: Pain and Glory
films.note: Antonio Banderas' Best Actor nomination was the hosts' happiest surprise of the morning.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Only a screenplay nomination for Rian Johnson. It missed Best Picture, as Amanda had predicted it would.

films.title: Ad Astra
films.note: A surprise Best Sound Editing nomination.

films.title: Honeyland
films.note: First film ever nominated for both International Feature and Documentary Feature in the same year.

films.title: Breakthrough
films.note: Diane Warren's song for the film, "I'm Standing With You," earned her 11th Original Song nomination.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Florence Pugh's Best Supporting Actress nomination.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: Jennifer Lopez missed a Best Supporting Actress nomination; Kathy Bates (Richard Jewell) took the presumed slot.

films.title: Dolemite Is My Name
films.note: Shut out entirely, including Ruth Carter's costume design.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Shut out entirely.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: Shut out entirely.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: Shut out entirely. The hosts said it's the film they'd add if the Academy allowed a 10th Best Picture slot.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Missed Best Editing.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: A24's only nomination all year, for Jarin Blaschke's cinematography.

films.title: Frozen II
films.note: Missed Best Animated Feature.

films.title: Klaus
films.note: Took the Best Animated Feature slot the hosts expected to go to Frozen II.

films.title: I Lost My Body
films.note: Nominated for Best Animated Feature.

films.title: Toy Story 4
films.note: Nominated for Best Animated Feature.

films.title: Atlantics
films.note: Missed Best International Feature; replaced by Corpus Christi.

films.title: Corpus Christi
films.note: Took the International Feature slot over Senegal's submission, Atlantics.

films.title: Apollo 11
films.note: A shock Best Documentary Feature omission.

films.title: American Factory
films.note: Sean's pick to win Best Documentary Feature.

films.title: One Child Nation
films.note: Amanda's most disappointing documentary snub.

films.title: The Edge of Democracy
films.note: A Brazilian Best Documentary Feature nominee.

films.title: Harriet
films.note: Cynthia Erivo's Best Actress nomination was the only acting nod for a performer of color this year.

films.title: Richard Jewell
films.note: Kathy Bates' Best Supporting Actress nomination.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Greta Gerwig's Best Director snub left zero women in the category, despite the film's Best Picture and Adapted Screenplay nominations.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: At the Critics' Choice Awards, Roman Griffin Davis beat co-star Archie Yates for best young performer.

films.title: Judy
films.note: Renée Zellweger's Critics' Choice win for playing Judy Garland renewed the hosts' debate over her casting.

films.title: Bridget Jones's Diary
films.note: Cited by Amanda as proof Renée Zellweger can carry a role, against complaints she was miscast in Judy.

films: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
